ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a dedicated Italian lobster bisque entry; closest parent 'lobster bisque' (FOODON_03302050) used as the nearest defensible anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific lobster bisque code; A0CKP (crustacean-based soup) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code exists for lobster bisque as a prepared dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of lobster bisque is approximately 300 g (one bowl), containing succulent lobster meat in a rich cream and tomato-based broth.

nutrient_source.title: Lobster bisque, restaurant-prepared — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods composite estimate
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA entry for Italian-style lobster bisque exists. Values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for cooked lobster (NDB 15147), cream-based seafood bisque (NDB 06016), and mirepoix vegetables, weighted to reflect the described recipe (lobster meat, tomato, carrot, onion, celery, basil, cream). Energy and macros are cross-checked against USDA FoodData Central entry FDC ID 171975 (lobster, cooked) and FDC ID 173420 (cream of shrimp soup, condensed, as analogue). Nutrient values represent a plausible per-100 g estimate for a restaurant-prepared cream-enriched lobster bisque.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for lobster bisque. GI estimated at 38 based on the closest analogous food: cream of tomato soup (GI ~38, University of Sydney GI Database). The bisque is a cream-enriched, protein-rich, low-carbohydrate seafood soup with mirepoix vegetables; the fat and protein content further attenuates glycemic response. Glycemic load per 300 g serving = (38 × 4.0 × 3) / 100 = 4.6 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~380 mg per 100 g; ~1140 mg per 300 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit lobster bisque to small portions or avoid it, as a single restaurant serving can contribute approximately 50% of the recommended daily sodium intake of 2000 mg; requesting a reduced-sodium preparation or consuming a half-portion is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~38) and low carbohydrate content (~4 g per 100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Lobster bisque has a low glycemic index and low glycemic load per serving, making it a relatively safe choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; however, the cream content contributes saturated fat and calories, so portion control and monitoring of total energy intake remain important.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~2.3 g per 100 g) and cholesterol (~48 mg per 100 g) from cream and lobster
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume lobster bisque in moderation due to its saturated fat and cholesterol content from cream and shellfish; the lobster provides beneficial omega-3 fatty acids and lean protein, but the high sodium content also warrants caution in those with concurrent hypertension or heart failure.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bisque di Aragosta is an Italian-style lobster bisque — a rich, velvety, cream-enriched seafood soup featuring succulent lobster meat simmered in a tomato-based broth with a classic mirepoix of chopped carrot, onion, and celery, finished with fresh basil. The dish originates from the French bisque tradition but is prepared here in an Italian culinary style, as served at Pasta Fresca Da Salvatore in Singapore. The texture is smooth and luxurious, with a deep umami flavour from the lobster shells and stock, balanced by the acidity of tomato and the sweetness of the aromatic vegetables. Nutritionally, lobster bisque is a moderate-energy dish (approximately 72 kcal per 100 g) with a meaningful protein contribution (~5.2 g per 100 g) from the lobster, moderate fat from cream (~4.1 g per 100 g, of which ~2.3 g is saturated), and low carbohydrate (~4.0 g per 100 g). The glycemic index is low (~38), making it suitable for blood glucose management, though the sodium content (~380 mg per 100 g) is a concern for individuals with hypertension. Lobster is a source of vitamin B12, zinc, selenium, and phosphorus. The dish is not suitable for those with crustacean or dairy allergies. It is typically served as a starter or light main course in fine-dining Italian restaurants.
common_user_queries: How many calories in lobster bisque?
Is lobster bisque good for diabetics?
What is the sodium content of lobster bisque?
What allergens are in lobster bisque?
What is the glycemic index of lobster bisque?

confidence_reason: No exact USDA or approved primary source entry exists for Italian-style lobster bisque as a prepared restaurant dish. Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for cooked lobster, cream-based seafood bisque analogues, and mirepoix vegetables. GI is estimated from the closest analogous food (cream of tomato soup, GI ~38) from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component-level USDA data is high quality and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Food group corrected from 'Grains and staples' (input) to 'Fish and seafood', which accurately reflects the primary ingredient (lobster). 2. Canonical English food name set to 'Lobster Bisque'; 'Bisque di Aragosta' is the Italian-language menu name used at Pasta Fresca Da Salvatore, Singapore, and is preserved as the primary romanized alias. 3. Nutrient values are estimated composites; a direct laboratory analysis of this specific restaurant dish would be required for high-confidence values. 4. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reliably reported in the USDA SR Legacy seafood bisque analogue entries used for estimation. 5. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the primary USDA analogue sources used for this estimation. 6. Trans fat is reported as 0.0 g per 100 g; this is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy data for the cream and lobster components — no industrial trans fats are present in this preparation. 7. GI of 38 is an analogous estimate based on cream of tomato soup from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for lobster bisque was found in the peer-reviewed literature. Glycemic load per 300 g serving = (38 × 4.0 × 3) / 100 = 4.6 g. 8. This is an Italian cuisine dish; multilingual naming rules for Italian cuisine do not mandate Chinese, Malay, Tamil, or Hindi local names — those fields are appropriately null. The romanized Italian name 'Bisque di Aragosta' is captured in romanized_local and aliases. 9. Halal status is marked false as lobster bisque contains cream (dairy) and the halal status of the specific restaurant preparation is unverified. 10. FoodOn URI FOODON_03302050 is used as the closest parent; reviewers should verify this URI against the current FoodOn release as ontology versioning may affect code validity.
